text: The crag does not need his scouts to tell him where the disruption originates, he can
feel it in his bones.
The runes along his forearms ache where the frequency bites, not enough to stop him,
enough to irritate.
He lift his war club and the runes along its length slow pattern, a new configuration,
one he has not used in this campaign.
The air around him thickens, bullets fired at his chest slow to a crawl, hanging suspended
in front of his fur armor, then dropped the snow like dead insects.
His elite guard forms a wedge behind him.
They move through the disrupted zone untouched because his spell operates on a different
frequency than the emitter can reach.
He is adapting.
Adapting in real time in the middle of a battle, rewriting his own circuitry while walking
forward.
Tanaka sees it through her binoculars.
She sees a single massive figure walking through a storm of gunfire without slowing down.
She sees the elite wedge driving toward the mid-it like a spear tip aimed at the heart
of her defense.
She drops the binoculars and runs toward the emitter position.
If Krag destroys it, every neanderthal on the field regains full room capacity in seconds.
The line will not just break, it will cease to exist.
